> ⚠️ **Work in Progress**: This library is still under construction and contains **bugs and missing features**. Use in production environments is not recommended.


## Shapes

The following shapes are supported by Pangolin:

##### 0-dimensional shapes:
- [`Point`](#point): A point in the plane.

##### 1-dimensional shapes:
- [`Segment`](#segment): Unoriented straight line segment.
- [`OrientedSegment`](#oriented-segment): Oriented straight line segment.
- [`Line`](#line) Infinite straight line.
- [`OrientedLine`](#oriented-line) Infinite oriented straight line.
- [`Ray`](#ray) Half-line.
- [`MonotoneChain`](#monotone-chain) Weakly x-monotone polyline.
- [`Polyline`](#polyline) Polygonal chain, possibly self-intersecting.

##### 2-dimensional shapes:
- [`Halfplane`](#half-plane) A straight line and all points on one side of it.
- [`Triangle`](#triangle) Unoriented triangle.
- [`Rectangle`](#rectangle) Axis-aligned rectangle.
- [`Disk`](#disk) A circle with its interior.
- [`Polygon`](#polygon) Simple polygon.
- [`Convex`](#convex) Convex polygon.
- [`HalfplaneIntersection`](#halfplane-intersection) Intersection of half-planes; convex but possibly unbounded or empty.

All shapes are template classes with a parameter that is a [`Point`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Point.html "Two-dimensional point with optional label payload.") type, with `pgl::Point<int>` as default:

```C++
pgl::Segment si = {1,2,3,4}; // Same as pgl::Segment<pgl::Point<int>>
pgl::Segment<pgl::Point<double>> sd = {1.0,1.5,2.0,2.5};
```

It is often convenient to define the types you use more often:

```
using Point = pgl::Point<double>;
using Segment = pgl::Segment<Point>;
using Triangle = pgl::Triangle<Point>;
```

There are many [predicates](shape_methods.md#predicates) and [other methods](shape_methods.md) supported by all shapes, such as `intersects`, `contains`, `squaredDistance`, `distanceL1`, translation, and scaling.
Shapes may be degenerate, for example when some of their defining points are equal. The behavior of geometric operations on degenerate shapes is undefined. However, degenerate shapes may safely be constructed and are often constructed by the default constructor that sets all points to the origin.

Shapes are grouped into a polymorphic class [`Shape`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Shape.html "Runtime variant wrapper over the supported primitive shapes.") that use `std::variant` for polymorphism.

```C++
pgl::Shape p = pgl::Point(3,7);
pgl::Shape s = pgl::Segment(1,4,2,9);
pgl::Shape r = pgl::Rectangle(1,4,2,9);
if (r.contains(p))
    std::cout << r << " contains " << p << std::endl;
if (r.intersects(s))
    std::cout << r << " intersects " << s << std::endl;
```

All shapes contain their boundaries (that is, they are closed in the topological sense). The boundary of a shape is the *manifold boundary*, that is:

- A point has no boundary.
- The boundary of a 1-dimensional shape is the set of (at most two) extreme points of the curve. The boundary of a segment are its two vertices. The boundary of a ray is its one vertex. A line has no boundary.
- The boundary of a 2-dimensional shape is defined in the usual way. The boundary of a triangle is its perimeter, the boundary of a halfplane is the line that defines it.


### Point

The [`Point`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Point.html "Two-dimensional point with optional label payload.") class template defines a point with x and y coordinates. A point may optionally have a [label](types.md#point-label). A point has no boundary and has the point itself as the interior.

```C++
pgl::Point p = {7,9};
pgl::Point<double> q = {3.5,2.25};
pgl::Point<int,std::string> c = {3,5,"center"};
```

You can read and change the coordinates of a point `p` as `p[0]` and `p[1]` or [`p.x()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Point.html#ad4a835037f85566d87811a0c23716ce1 "Returns the x coordinate.") and [`p.y()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Point.html#adddf838c3aab69fee39bf5ffb847f72d "Returns the y coordinate."). You can also iterate through the coordinates.

```C++
pgl::Point p;
p.x() = 7;
p[1] = 9;
for(int coord : p) std::cout << coord << ' ';
std::cout << p << std::endl;
// Output: 7 9 (7,9)
```

A point has methods:
- [`p.swapped()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Point.html#a313ef4ae28a7d9f0405b6dd455775120 "Returns the point with x and y swapped."): Returns the point with x and y coordinates swapped.
- [`p.dual()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Point.html#a6eff780dd37bb234d722052fa0453f5d "Returns the dual Line."): Returns the dual line $y = ax - b$ for a point $(a,b)$.
- [`p.polar()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Point.html#adfb9d9182a567e05d249f6ce4a124f7d "Returns the polar Line."): Returns the polar line $ax + by = 1$ for a point $(a,b)$. Undefined for the origin.

- Other methods: [`bbox`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Point.html#aa06f32e7c4b67bca6c5496338013d099 "Returns the bounding box of the point."), ### Segment

The [`Segment`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html "Unoriented closed segment between two endpoints plus optional segment label.") class template defines an unoriented straight line segment. The segment always stores the endpoints in increasing order.

```C++
pgl::Segment s(1,2,3,4), t(3,4,1,2);
if (s == t)
    std::cout << s << " == " << t << std::endl;
// Output: (1,2)--(3,4) == (1,2)--(3,4)
```

You can read the two endpoints of a segment `s` as `s[0]` and `s[1]`. You cannot directly change the endpoints. You can also iterate through the endpoints.

```C++
pgl::Segment s(3,4,1,2);
for(size_t i : {0,1}) std::cout << s[i] << ' ';
for(pgl::Point p : s) std::cout << p << ' ';
// Output: (1,2) (3,4) (1,2) (3,4)
```

The interior of a segment is all the segment except the two endpoints.
```C++
pgl::Segment s(1,0,5,0), t(2,0,2,3);
if (s.intersects(t)) std::cout << "Intersect!";
if (!s.interiorsIntersect(t)) std::cout << " Interiors do not intersect!\n";
// Output: Intersect! Interiors do not intersect!
```

A segment `s` has methods such as:

- [`s.midpoint()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a6ba4f70a48483f85bb36651d9fa16275 "Returns the midpoint of the segment."): Returns the midpoint. Uses division by 2, so make sure that the coordinates are even or a non-integer type is used. Notice that floating point handles divisions by powers of 2 exactly.
- [`s.length()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a8c2640b1515b891d3458b89e2bf852ac "Returns the Euclidean length."): Returns `s[0].distance(s[1])`.
- [`s.squaredLength()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#ae7e06f6bf41a97e0b9a2e2af80eb442d "Returns the squared Euclidean length."): Returns `s[0].squaredDistance(s[1])`.
- [`s.isDegenerate()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a2d653f0f6dfd0664e8afe91816e262c1 "Returns whether both endpoints coincide."): Returns `s.length() == 0`.
- `s.isPoint()` / `s.getIfPoint()`: Whether the segment collapses to a single point (all defining points equal), and that point as a `std::optional<PointType>`.
- `s.isUndefined()`: Always `false`: a degenerate segment is always a point.
- [`s.isVertical()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#ad93d1387983c7189e9e201382111c87c "Returns whether the segment is vertical."): Returns `s[0].x() == s[1].x()`.
- [`s.isHorizontal()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a6903b76ac4686b9335f6f116c53f322d "Returns whether the segment is horizontal."): Returns `s[0].y() == s[1].y()`.
- [`s.containsEndpoint(p)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a90315b916a47d2356ff1793db49f9d63 "Returns whether the given point is one endpoint."): Returns `s[0] == p || s[1] == p`
- [`s.collinear(t)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a98ea3f482d52dec230b1a6ff2832af79 "Returns whether the given point lies on the supporting line."): Returns whether `s` and `t` are on the same line, where `t` may be a point or another segment.
- [`s.slope()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a6104dac8ebf1ae689aad41031b1c853e "Returns the slope of the segment."): Returns `(s[1].y()-s[0].y()) / (s[1].x()-s[0].x())`.
- [`s.parallel(t)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#af8c2ca1c497f63c2d7fdd9c984201ed2 "Returns whether another segment is parallel to this one."): Returns whether `s` and `t` have the same slope, but without using division. Here, `t` may be a segment, oriented segment, line, ray, or oriented line.
- [`s.yAtX(x)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#af4487113eb0a0b25129415b203d39b27 "Returns the value of the y coordinate for a given x, if it exists."): Returns an `std::optional` with the value of the segment y coordinate at the given coordinate `x`.
- [`s.xAtY(y)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#afc0fd6524c9dd5b450e9ca6393df79d9 "Returns the value of the x coordinate for a given y, if it exists."): Returns an `std::optional` with the value of the segment x coordinate at the given coordinate `y`.

It knows how to convert itself with an explicit cast to:
- `(pgl::Line) s` or [`s.asLine()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a17e749637dd68e87c00a49b75e80f958 "Returns the supporting line."): Returns the line that contains `s`.

[`asPolyline`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#aa1cf93051c0a3968e6b42dc9a557fee1 "Returns the segment as a two-vertex polyline."), [`lengthLInf`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a403c0f01a4a06c9a66870d26ee3fbb91 "Returns the Chebyshev length."), [`max`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a7ed2bf58648e2eb9b29c349b4762aaca "Returns the largest stored endpoint."), [`min`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a8ca634e3fec2026ef7a18c4a2652667d "Returns the smallest stored endpoint."), [`orientedEdges`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#aeb3dab6fe571994ba130dc4f005deeb5 "Returns the unique oriented boundary edge in canonical order."), [`pointInside`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#ac381a6cbc94661fbb3f2e602f3999c78 "Returns a point inside the segment."), [`pointInsideInteriorContainedIn`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a4b32791ffe9422eae66290b99520b3f3 "Tests whether some point in this shape's relative interior lies in the strict interior of shape."), [`rotate90`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a3fd8c134074fbf2636aa9743ccbf030c "Rotates the segment by 90k degrees around the origin in place."), [`rotated90`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a9e5459b86e0eaf6f007d26681a73a240 "Returns the segment rotated by 90k degrees around the origin."), [`scaleDownX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#aeb711153f0e2394cf4c7e7e84d2f5fa5 "Divides the segment's x-coordinates by a divisor in place."), [`scaleDownY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a5ba2ee3d871dedf12d5a92ecc79ad85b "Divides the segment's y-coordinates by a divisor in place."), [`scaleUpX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#ac0874880eb0fa131702a2857aa0638f4 "Multiplies the segment's x-coordinates by a factor in place."), [`scaleUpY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a695297c93ef05dcaf56affb98c382ff8 "Multiplies the segment's y-coordinates by a factor in place."), [`scaledDownX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#ac4e8d3430d7104397cb7c9986edcd3c2 "Returns the segment with its x-coordinates divided by a divisor."), [`scaledDownY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a64ec4d17a0155867dc878685acb99d2c "Returns the segment with its y-coordinates divided by a divisor."), [`scaledUpX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a30cf555d792ffdd84c962b05eb54f63a "Returns the segment with its x-coordinates multiplied by a factor."), [`scaledUpY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a7601f099064cb93721e95d03b2f4371f "Returns the segment with its y-coordinates multiplied by a factor."), [`separates`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a830c2dec5f515ba822e95e19229565c5 "Tests whether removing this shape disconnects the other shape (B∖A is disconnected)."), [`size`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a889dfc74b64baa67e97bc4c7dd413a6a "Returns the number of endpoints (always 2)."), [`squaredDistance`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a96dd962e8a2361d1cb0bc7a32ee5af48 "Returns the squared Euclidean distance to the given shape."), [`squaredHausdorffDistance`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#ae7bba7fbc2fefc7de46ce8a32a4f4c02 "Returns the squared Hausdorff distance to another segment."), [`twiceArea`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#add64cac458c92a18bfdfbb87363408c3 "Returns twice the area of the segment."), [`vertices`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a175b49d33518dc20795f0bdd752d2313 "Returns the two endpoints in canonical order."), [`verticesContain`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html#a08097be84764754c35aac6cc6f1d5d96 "Returns whether one endpoint equals the given point.").


### Oriented Segment

The [`OrientedSegment`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html "Directed segment preserving source-to-target order plus optional segment label.") class template defines an oriented straight line segment. The user chooses the order of the two endpoints, which are named [`source`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a2ed4278927ccabd73b1d99c8f8903f10 "Returns the source endpoint.") and [`target`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a6ed02b384a6f31fd6b4c8671286f7231 "Returns the target endpoint."), respectively.

```C++
pgl::OrientedSegment s(1,2,3,4), t(3,4,1,2);
if (s != t)
    std::cout << s << " != " << t << std::endl;
// Output: (1,2)->(3,4) != (3,4)->(1,2)
```

You can read the two endpoints of a segment `s` as `s[0]` and `s[1]` or [`s.source()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a2ed4278927ccabd73b1d99c8f8903f10 "Returns the source endpoint.") and [`s.target()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a6ed02b384a6f31fd6b4c8671286f7231 "Returns the target endpoint."). You can directly change the endpoints. You can also iterate through the endpoints.

```C++
pgl::OrientedSegment s(1,2,3,4);
s[0][0] = 5;
s.target().x() = 7;
std::cout << s << std::endl;
// Output: (5,2)->(7,4)
```

An oriented segment `s` has all methods of the [`Segment`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html "Unoriented closed segment between two endpoints plus optional segment label.") class, with the only difference being for the slope, which may be negative:

- [`s.midpoint()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a9fcc978c0e1b9d9053f2182da496db20 "Returns the midpoint of the segment."): Returns the midpoint. Uses division by 2, so make sure that the coordinates are even or a non-integer type is used. Notice that floating point handles divisions by powers of 2 exactly.
- [`s.length()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a003b0ff77fdeaff80dfcd5591f884a8f "Returns the Euclidean length."): Returns `s[0].distance(s[1])`.
- [`s.squaredLength()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a9314e8f0decc3ea85b2ec04a6bb845d8 "Returns the squared Euclidean length."): Returns `s[0].squaredDistance(s[1])`.
- [`s.isDegenerate()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a4dc0c036962ec7a1a9142296de15e64f "Returns whether both endpoints coincide."): Returns `s.length() == 0`.
- `s.isPoint()` / `s.getIfPoint()`: Whether the segment collapses to a single point (all defining points equal), and that point as a `std::optional<PointType>`.
- `s.isUndefined()`: Always `false`: a degenerate segment is always a point.
- [`s.isVertical()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a7193013018c0600c23b9719e9a631247 "Returns whether the segment is vertical."): Returns `s[0].x() == s[1].x()`.
- [`s.isHorizontal()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#ac8af96509cf5232e92b04a02cedcfa46 "Returns whether the segment is horizontal."): Returns `s[0].y() == s[1].y()`.
- [`s.containsEndpoint(p)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a270cc8f80403c643fd94bc36e9ecff50 "Returns whether the given point is one endpoint."): Returns `s[0] == p || s[1] == p`
- [`s.collinear(t)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#aeb0cf7ae8f1880e3671599fd760f458b "Returns whether the given point is collinear with the oriented segment."): Returns whether `s` and `t` are on the same line, where `t` may be a point or another segment.
- [`s.slope()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a2e104f74709a9c280784291df2e868d0 "Returns the slope of the segment."): Returns `(s[1].y()-s[0].y()) / (s[1].x()-s[0].x())`.
- [`s.parallel(t)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#afba1cca649f1edc93dc94bd69be42ca5 "Returns whether the given segment is parallel to the oriented segment."): Returns whether `s` and `t` have the same slope, but without using division. Here, `t` may be a segment, oriented segment, line, ray, or oriented line.
- [`s.yAtX(x)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a8f66de4ed57d7708e522a0bb1f77b566 "Returns the value of the y coordinate for a given x, if it exists."): Returns an `std::optional` with the value of the segment y coordinate at the given coordinate `x`.
- [`s.xAtY(y)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#ae08fd372179c6d2b5d146435c2e3abc8 "Returns the value of the x coordinate for a given y, if it exists."): Returns an `std::optional` with the value of the segment x coordinate at the given coordinate `y`.

It also has:

- [`s.opposite()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#abd0d595b6568119fd0a07a4ef5f4250b "Returns the opposite orientation of the same geometric segment."): Returns the segment with source and target interchanged.
- [`s.orientation(p)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a531355c630b3b171d3e7ab41f6842149 "Returns the orientation sign of a point with respect to the segment."): Given a point `p`, returns the orientation sign of `s[0],s[1],p`: null when they are collinear, negative when `s` sees `p` to its right, and positive when `s` sees `p` to its left.
- [`s.rightHalfplane()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a16de5b4095b9e58bbd2caa405fe44a7c "Returns the half-plane on the right of the segment direction."): Returns the half-plane defined by all points `p` such that `s.orientation(p) <= 0`.
- [`s.leftHalfplane()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a848ba0978471a2ad0df5e246ecd2d077 "Returns the half-plane on the left of the segment direction."): Returns the half-plane defined by all points `p` such that `s.orientation(p) >= 0`.

It knows how to convert itself with an explicit cast to:
- `(pgl::OrientedLine) s` or [`s.asOrientedLine()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a32abd5495bfe740ffcfaead86078214b "Returns the oriented supporting line."): Returns the line that contains `s` and has the same orientation.
- `(pgl::Ray) s`  or [`s.asRay()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#ab9b6246fac158c6af6fc77f20b713a04 "Returns the ray with the same source and direction."): Returns the half-line that contains `s` and has the same source.

- Other methods: [`area`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a728b6cd39d9cf4e4e6aad12b1758f32e "Returns the area of the segment."), [`asLine`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#af7700575ec9d0865ab559d3a70b3ab94 "Returns the supporting line without orientation."), [`asSegment`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a839015c7197fe69821533cda01d8136a "Returns the segment without orientation."), [`bbox`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a27d265989a331167765f44b9d859a6c5 "Returns the bounding box of the oriented segment."), [`begin`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a6312e986668621bd484835fab5843831 "Returns an iterator to the source endpoint."), [`boundaryContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a31c81c01b377355dbfcd9dd4f02f3fad "Tests whether this shape's boundary contains the other shape (∂A ⊇ B)."), [`cbegin`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a37f0f59c2d9e82d9c1b3a1795455332f "Returns an iterator to the source endpoint."), [`cend`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a916becc1b4b29308a43d5225c78a091c "Returns an iterator past the target endpoint."), [`contains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#abaafa2a3f5387d07b4797c2933ea4aee "Tests whether this shape contains the other shape (A ⊇ B)."), [`containsCollinear`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#ab99a981d4cfbe2087e55a97b52eab145 "Returns whether the segment contains the given point that is collinear with the segment."), [`crosses`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a20c07ae3def1d7976af11cb18f0e5cbf "Tests whether the two shapes mutually separate each other (each disconnects the other)."), [`diameter`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#adb88cba4f4637968187c78fddaabaf16 "Returns an unordered segment defining the diameter."), [`distanceL1`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a3b7ea89d712f539e67e785f829d2f6f5 "Returns the Manhattan (L1) distance to the given shape."), [`distanceLInf`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a90dfd6d9b702d80a2757598fd9f7009f "Returns the Chebyshev (LInf) distance to the given shape."), [`edges`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a8b7ee1fb46bf24ee60fde12e86c33e04 "Returns the unique geometric edge of the oriented segment."), [`end`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a70dd90317f2eac7f03d1d276514826f5 "Returns an iterator past the target endpoint."), [`fbox`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a30cbae58290e89e188e6b751599384d7 "Returns a bounding box of the oriented segment with floating point coordinates."), [`get`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a7d84f893e981bf010eeff2e81e4e4d91 "Cyclic access: same as operator[] but index is taken modulo size(); negative indices wrap from the end."), [`hausdorffDistanceL1`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a24eca87c73872ebef717020ba6b87517 "Returns the Manhattan (L1) Hausdorff distance to the given shape."), [`hausdorffDistanceLInf`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#af6d974417644e3bc7cad31abd71ab70f "Returns the Chebyshev (LInf) Hausdorff distance to the given shape."), [`index`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a0e85f32bac2d7392328e751be0a1e1f3 "Returns the smallest index i with (*this)[i] == point, or -1 if no [`verticesContain`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedSegment.html#a247b09048136f7092ba4642d734943d6 "Returns whether one endpoint equals the given point.").

### EmptyShape

Represents the empty set. Its [`size()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1EmptyShape.html#a5bd45b3506aa274a0cd9f12ca257ab8f "Returns the number of vertices, always 0.") is 0, it intersects nothing, and it is contained in everything.

- Other methods: [`boundaryContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1EmptyShape.html#a7a6e4ac95bb087d71e7283647f642e44 "Tests whether this shape's boundary contains the other shape (∂A ⊇ B)."), [`contains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1EmptyShape.html#a0bf0800ed2c1c2262812110d3983f837 "Tests whether this shape contains the other shape (A ⊇ B)."), [`crosses`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1EmptyShape.html#af46b0a6534ef5aa5734ee3414a6067b7 "Tests whether the two shapes mutually separate each other (each disconnects the other)."), [`get`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1EmptyShape.html#a71080110672913245ba1ddbd5f31693a "Always throws: the empty shape has no vertices."), [`index`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1EmptyShape.html#a82ab1d0879b5b087a4f2f39e601aa9b9 "Always throws: the empty shape has no vertices."), [`interiorContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1EmptyShape.html#ade98d72ccebb9e8b64346365cbb5fc30 "Tests whether this shape's interior contains the other shape (A∖∂A ⊇ B)."), [`interiorsIntersect`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1EmptyShape.html#adc6cc1877378411a8d76af443d740de8 "Tests whether the interiors of the two shapes intersect ((A∖∂A) ∩ (B∖∂B) ≠ ∅)."), [`intersection`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1EmptyShape.html#a03b459e2e2d907a3e8425af0a01daa6a "Returns the intersection of the two shapes (A ∩ B), empty when they are disjoint."), [`intersects`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1EmptyShape.html#a545a5779a108a3bbb3ad673b1429dbb1 "Tests whether this shape and the other shape intersect (A ∩ B ≠ ∅)."), [`isDegenerate`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1EmptyShape.html#a8324a647c03113afcc4fc6a7f37656cb "The empty shape is never degenerate."), [`rotate90`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1EmptyShape.html#adb963a926ea9a2e87f97669dc2295875 "Rotates the empty shape by 90k degrees in place; a no-op."), [`rotated90`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1EmptyShape.html#a17d14a625d491a843fdce0549e1ec3f9 "Returns the empty shape rotated by 90k degrees; a no-op."), [`scaleDownX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1EmptyShape.html#a7b8ba57145832af1cef78466edd145b7 "Scales the empty shape's x-coordinates down in place; a no-op."), [`scaleDownY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1EmptyShape.html#acf269801d7a03a3e46610894a4bbd019 "Scales the empty shape's y-coordinates down in place; a no-op."), [`scaleUpX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1EmptyShape.html#afce27cf44d531544527dc19551f155e0 "Scales the empty shape's x-coordinates up in place; a no-op."), [`scaleUpY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1EmptyShape.html#a8a16ce16042ee7a51be420925c9c4d16 "Scales the empty shape's y-coordinates up in place; a no-op."), [`scaledDownX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1EmptyShape.html#a631cf743632c44f8764636503f393eaf "Returns the empty shape with its x-coordinates scaled down; a no-op."), [`scaledDownY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1EmptyShape.html#a9af1863b2cb9325bc191f28bb789f699 "Returns the empty shape with its y-coordinates scaled down; a no-op."), [`scaledUpX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1EmptyShape.html#ab378e5c1cead7c8b6de619df630882a3 "Returns the empty shape with its x-coordinates scaled up; a no-op."), [`scaledUpY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1EmptyShape.html#abc49fb8afaa1cdaf71d18d2aa09db085 "Returns the empty shape with its y-coordinates scaled up; a no-op."), [`separates`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1EmptyShape.html#a1ff3719fc56858cf597717f6c72e9c7b "Tests whether removing this shape disconnects the other shape (B∖A is disconnected).").

### Line

The class template [`Line`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html "Unoriented infinite line.") represents an infinite unoriented straight line. A line is stored as any two points it contains, but two lines defined by two distinct collinear points always compare equal. The two points are stored in increasing order.

```C++
pgl::Line l1(1,2,3,4), l2(2,3,1,2);
if (l1 == l2)
    std::cout << l1 << " == " << l2 << std::endl;
// Output: -(1,2)--(3,4)- == -(1,2)--(2,3)-
```

The defining points may be accessed as in a segment and may not be changed directly. The interior of a line is the whole line, so [`contains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a0f408aa140c9bd52f34b2b7569e3d32d "Tests whether this shape contains the other shape (A ⊇ B).") and [`interiorContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#ada10a674bd914aa57d211add8a53dfe8 "Tests whether this shape's interior contains the other shape (A∖∂A ⊇ B).") are equivalent.

A line `l` has some additional methods such as:

- [`l.isDegenerate()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#aea48143a2889d294c15306a18bbc382f "Returns whether the defining points coincide."): Returns `l[0] == l[1]`.
- [`l.isVertical()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a4b206c7339d9e8dd2c13c41de28f2d12 "Returns whether the line is vertical."): Returns `l[0].x() == l[1].x()`.
- [`l.isHorizontal()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a066e7201c620f1ce7311ca130b6bd672 "Returns whether the line is horizontal."): Returns `l[0].y() == l[1].y()`.
- [`l.slope()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a65b09754cb121f239f3133a082fbdaeb "Returns the slope of the line."): Returns `(l[1].y()-l[0].y()) / (l[1].x()-l[0].x())`.
- [`l.parallel(t)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a4fba0a6b1449af25c123a338628d8ae5 "Returns whether another line is parallel to this line."): Returns whether `l` and `t` have the same slope, but without using division. Here, `t` may be a segment, oriented segment, line, ray, or oriented line.
- [`l.halfplaneAbove()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a68570dbd34b227416c066766c61249ef "Returns the half-plane geometrically above this line."): Returns the half-plane defined by all points `p` that are above the line (larger y-coordinate). If the line is vertical, then it returns the half-plane with smaller x-coordinate. In other words, it returns the half-plane defined by all points `p` such that `pgl::OrientedSegment(l[0],l[1]).orientation(p) >= 0`, noticing that `l[0] < l[1]`.
- [`l.halfplaneBelow()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#aecad497c330e3cfdeac70d0b54bebf3a "Returns the half-plane geometrically below this line."): Returns the half-plane containing `l` and not [`halfplaneAbove`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a68570dbd34b227416c066766c61249ef "Returns the half-plane geometrically above this line.").
- [`l.dual()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#aa72029be2b77416b240bb2511a8e6e57 "Returns the dual point."): Returns the point $(a,b)$ such that `l` is defined by $y = ax - b$. Undefined behavior for vertical lines.
- [`l.polar()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a9a9a9d07c0d3aafcc6b273bc70dfbb0a "Returns the polar point."): Returns the point $(a,b)$ such that `l` is defined by $ax + by = 1$. Undefined behavior for lines that contain the origin.
- [`l.yAtX(x)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a7788ca87e20897f7a5a4efe74fd7055c "Returns the y-coordinate of the line at a given x-coordinate, if defined."): Returns the value of the line y coordinate at the given coordinate `x`.
- [`l.xAtY(y)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a70a94d1e1a7c7bd1108ae00c273fde85 "Returns the x-coordinate of the line at a given y-coordinate, if defined."): Returns the value of the line x coordinate at the given coordinate `y`.

- Other methods: [`area`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a852fafcdfd7c159cea73c207b334f2c3 "Returns the area of the line."), [`asSegmentFor`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a3b2e56ce8108ec3b88fadc88996d2998 "Returns the segment that intersects r the same way as this line."), [`begin`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a4522c14c252a3093c83fcc5381ae4801 "Returns an iterator to the first defining point."), [`boundaryContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a36cd0d13335330379612fbd0a72f0284 "Tests whether this shape's boundary contains the other shape (∂A ⊇ B)."), [`cbegin`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#ac92427a493388e54d07c8a51671fb069 "Returns an iterator to the first defining point."), [`cend`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a173527bfe987fec13c62e0402b2d2757 "Returns an iterator past the last defining point."), [`collinear`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a4cfa4e78e3e5b32c03d28c3982d10fad "Returns whether the line interior contains the given point."), [`crosses`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a76eec7b766eecde475537034df31286c "Tests whether the two shapes mutually separate each other (each disconnects the other)."), [`distanceL1`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a151fcdf3d89877652115f0aef06ce8d1 "Returns the Manhattan (L1) distance to the given shape."), [`distanceLInf`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a8a38cba848b1ccad656ed53868cc8517 "Returns the Chebyshev (LInf) distance to the given shape."), [`dualCoordinates`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a1b4df1c4b503f41c70511df39debb3bf "Returns normalized dual-line coordinates for the supporting line."), [`end`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#aaebdb39d99bba13b42a26a3a91b679a9 "Returns an iterator past the last defining point."), [`get`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#aa78d70c2e1b07bee3ba4eba2083c29c7 "Cyclic access: same as operator[] but index is taken modulo size(); negative indices wrap from the end."), [`index`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#acb7f7b3f4940841503855d09b8947c23 "Returns the smallest index i with (*this)[i] == point, or -1 if no defining point equals point."), [`interiorsIntersect`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a8ad8361f30c1fb7b9549826912bd2dca "Tests whether the interiors of the two shapes intersect ((A∖∂A) ∩ (B∖∂B) ≠ ∅)."), [`intersection`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#abde7287285f241414eb264250503aeb8 "Returns the intersection of the two shapes (A ∩ B), empty when they are disjoint."), [`intersects`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a51dbd1d954a537292ae236cd5b67aad2 "Tests whether this shape and the other shape intersect (A ∩ B ≠ ∅)."), [`label`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a8f95fdfc54e852f2be721b09ee67b33c "Returns the line label."), [`max`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a320576b5b0950794eb5994b2b32faa03 "Returns the largest stored defining point."), [`min`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a5143b9b0c107fecab04a397b47203156 "Returns the smallest stored defining point."), [`pointInside`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a02a7f68895d3822c49025defca738341 "Returns a point inside the line."), [`pointInsideInteriorContainedIn`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a55ef7d8571612b32b35f722b255ee8ac "Tests whether some point in this shape's relative interior lies in the strict interior of shape."), [`rotate90`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a2e89314127bafa4e01da38c9b74e3882 "Rotates the line by 90k degrees around the origin in place."), [`rotated90`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a7aeca6a269be181179e5b450f59898a7 "Returns the line rotated by 90k degrees around the origin."), [`scaleDownX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#acd5d61a9bcc4fb8ab5ca6f73b0f51e31 "Divides the line's x-coordinates by a divisor in place."), [`scaleDownY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#aa70e7239057ff98a7d847ef57ffed83c "Divides the line's y-coordinates by a divisor in place."), [`scaleUpX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a709b2751e41450732da6957219ac31ef "Multiplies the line's x-coordinates by a factor in place."), [`scaleUpY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#af67fedd5c1d688bb6e4bae226538f95a "Multiplies the line's y-coordinates by a factor in place."), [`scaledDownX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a38451225a7e1adf9a0f0df5837fcec9a "Returns the line with its x-coordinates divided by a divisor."), [`scaledDownY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#afa660ffc7505164effa8a082cc983059 "Returns the line with its y-coordinates divided by a divisor."), [`scaledUpX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#ad729ec6057d0a597cdc40339ab877cce "Returns the line with its x-coordinates multiplied by a factor."), [`scaledUpY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a6e276431cbef396ca4c3b23c83d95f09 "Returns the line with its y-coordinates multiplied by a factor."), [`separates`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a1c62c1282194fcd3c8004b44c1c27d40 "Tests whether removing this shape disconnects the other shape (B∖A is disconnected)."), [`size`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a87a202d457d89f32911b6dc811be6361 "Returns the number of defining points (always 2)."), [`squaredDistance`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a6fabdb5bd2f6def14eece9e20d295455 "Returns the squared Euclidean distance to the given shape."), [`twiceArea`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#ad94a95afb14713ff5d909063f5e91865 "Returns twice the area of the line."), [`verticesContain`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html#a91b2e25a94740265188b175967c14f8c "Returns whether the given point is one of the stored defining points.").


### Oriented Line

The class template [`OrientedLine`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html "Directed infinite line with left/right side semantics plus optional line label.") represents an infinite oriented straight line. An oriented line is stored as any two points it contains but the order matters as the line is oriented from the source to the target point. Two lines defined by two distinct collinear points compare equal if the points are in the same lexicographical order.

```C++
pgl::OrientedLine l1(1,2,3,4), l2(2,3,1,2);
if (l1 != l2)
    std::cout << l1 << " != " << l2;
l2 = l2.opposite();
if (l1 == l2)
    std::cout << l1 << " == " << l2;
// Output: -(1,2)--(3,4)-> != -(2,3)--(1,2)->
//         -(1,2)--(3,4)-> == -(1,2)--(2,3)->
```

The defining points may be accessed as in an oriented segment and may be changed directly. The interior of an oriented line is the whole oriented line, so [`contains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#a197af38d52a8dec41b6f3d0850e8a7ef "Tests whether this shape contains the other shape (A ⊇ B).") and [`interiorContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#a9b9427e23d27587f59c32a60dfd6e8ba "Tests whether this shape's interior contains the other shape (A∖∂A ⊇ B).") are equivalent.

An oriented line `l` has methods such as:

- [`l.isDegenerate()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#ab0a5530262e75a3f25423ea3f5bf14cd "Returns whether the defining points coincide."): Returns `l[0] == l[1]`.
- [`l.isVertical()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#add58c7c5ce15d56d0d142e7bc145d581 "Returns whether the line is vertical."): Returns `l[0].x() == l[1].x()`.
- [`l.isHorizontal()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#aed8a8d6c00d0366a910bc72a1f646040 "Returns whether the line is horizontal."): Returns `l[0].y() == l[1].y()`.
- [`l.opposite()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#a9bf9b108074f26a0943b5d4cc34d8a67 "Returns the opposite orientation of the same geometric line."): Returns the oriented line with source and target interchanged.
- [`l.slope()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#a31868e82121d74b3016766b5482ebff6 "Returns the slope of the line."): Returns `(l[1].y()-l[0].y()) / (l[1].x()-l[0].x())`, possibly negative.
- [`l.parallel(t)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#af3e4eb23abc25f758b37cb1f5d70a286 "Returns whether the given line is parallel to the oriented line."): Returns whether `l` and `t` have the same slope, but without using division. Here, `t` may be a segment, oriented segment, line, ray, or oriented line.
- [`l.halfplaneAbove()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#aeda2dc46e413a0d34b5cb2a127a29ba5 "Returns the half-plane geometrically above the supporting line."): Returns the half-plane defined by all points `p` that are above the line (larger y-coordinate). If the line is vertical, then it returns the half-plane with smaller x-coordinate. In other words, it returns the half-plane defined by all points `p` such that `pgl::OrientedSegment(l[0],l[1]).orientation(p) <= 0`, noticing that `l[0] < l[1]`.
- [`l.halfplaneBelow()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#ad35665c61b605d8b0057ae774367d7d6 "Returns the half-plane geometrically below the supporting line."): Returns the half-plane containing `l` and not [`halfplaneAbove`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#aeda2dc46e413a0d34b5cb2a127a29ba5 "Returns the half-plane geometrically above the supporting line.").
- [`l.orientation(p)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#aabcb4586ac2a8cc5c655eaa9f2208bca "Returns the orientation sign of a point with respect to the line."): Given a point `p`, returns the orientation sign of `l[0],l[1],p`: null when they are collinear, negative when `l` sees `p` to its right, and positive when `l` sees `p` to its left.
- [`l.rightHalfplane()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#a5b450be0b6c1ebcee8d26b805ba000fb "Returns the half-plane on the right of the oriented line."): Returns the half-plane defined by all points `p` such that `l.orientation(p) <= 0`.
- [`l.leftHalfplane()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#a3d80b03525b963c2255216fb6769ddd0 "Returns the half-plane on the left of the oriented line."): Returns the half-plane defined by all points `p` such that `l.orientation(p) >= 0`.
- [`l.yAtX(x)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#ad7299b16efd66c8a5e620684c240a96e "Returns the y-coordinate of the supporting line at a given x-coordinate, if defined."): Returns the value of the line y coordinate at the given coordinate `x`.
- [`l.xAtY(y)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#a9066c3e2a39cb45346775fe4f985ef72 "Returns the x-coordinate of the supporting line at a given y-coordinate, if defined."): Returns the value of the line x coordinate at the given coordinate `y`.

It knows how to convert itself with an explicit cast to:
- `(pgl::Line) l` or [`l.asLine()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#abb932f7bbe6ceb52cc031c12d8c4558b "Returns the line without orientation."): Returns the line without the orientation.

- Other methods: [`area`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#aa3fe0ee224bd4a89975fce8d13a51bbe "Returns the area of the line."), [`asOrientedSegmentFor`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#af83213672ebafb63f17c64d5138e2220 "Returns the oriented segment that intersects r the same way as this oriented line."), [`begin`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#a8fe10c1d6c78abcde21a22326ab16ae6 "Returns an iterator to the source defining point."), [`boundaryContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#a2e52ea375be1fb13b67e0256f7945ac0 "Tests whether this shape's boundary contains the other shape (∂A ⊇ B)."), [`cbegin`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#ab22cf551a336c59cd99d224be39d695d "Returns an iterator to the source defining point."), [`cend`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#a9ed5d604315cfd9c910665cf75226199 "Returns an iterator past the target defining point."), [`collinear`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#a65120372dbe5b2486ae04039b984472a "Returns whether the given point is collinear with the oriented line."), [`crosses`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#a0458bbebfaaa1841bf90a90cded19564 "Tests whether the two shapes mutually separate each other (each disconnects the other)."), [`crossingOrder`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#a68183d94c20884bb21f47134b6f42766 "Orders two lines by where they cross this oriented line."), [`distanceL1`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#acf5ee045bcc3eef55541dcd73577c966 "Returns the Manhattan (L1) distance to the given shape."), [`distanceLInf`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#ac383baef8ff418c69958b32deab46209 "Returns the Chebyshev (LInf) distance to the given shape."), [`end`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#af04ee6ccc6bf71e3107bf3365ba29073 "Returns an iterator past the target defining point."), [`get`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#af8f8a1643b96fa06d90612e56bd73541 "Cyclic access: same as operator[] but index is taken modulo size(); negative indices wrap from the end."), [`index`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#ab8976011bb7580e03e4811d42935209d "Returns the smallest index i with (*this)[i] == point, or -1 if no defining point equals point."), [`interiorsIntersect`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#ac0870879c32a5911c0702fb303c1b5c5 "Tests whether the interiors of the two shapes intersect ((A∖∂A) ∩ (B∖∂B) ≠ ∅)."), [`intersection`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#a5accc11603e7e8048e70ccd16d6c3f13 "Returns the intersection of the two shapes (A ∩ B), empty when they are disjoint."), [`intersects`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#ad5eebc41bc419dccadbc6e63cdc09f30 "Tests whether this shape and the other shape intersect (A ∩ B ≠ ∅)."), [`label`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#a1eaf9ae1db110af53ec64ad04270ad30 "Returns the line label."), [`min`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#adeebd042e968ec518a76dbe1cda3f8c9 "Returns the lexicographically smallest defining point."), [`pointInside`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#a3c73c7f60b7910bc45c49daec553312b "Returns a point inside the oriented line."), [`pointInsideInteriorContainedIn`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#a8a792e297b690c24cb5b9f8394c0dcd6 "Tests whether some point in this shape's relative interior lies in the strict interior of shape."), [`rotate90`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#a6ac5046221104fe666769956443bfcae "Rotates the oriented line by 90k degrees around the origin in place."), [`rotated90`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#abc039c9ba303040c5dd4376d542e1f84 "Returns the oriented line rotated by 90k degrees around the origin."), [`scaleDownX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#ac1489e9dbea1c9dca5fa565ae1c81b6e "Divides the line's x-coordinates by a divisor in place."), [`scaledDownY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#a9ee00df6878f79419aeb38a0a2de818a "Returns the line with its y-coordinates divided by a divisor."), [`verticesContain`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1OrientedLine.html#a7a6c854b76f6e0eadf672dee09b30091 "Returns whether the given point is one of the stored defining points.").


### Ray

The class template [`Ray`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html "Half-infinite line starting from one source point plus optional ray label.") represents a half-line. A ray is stored as its source endpoint and any other point it contains. Two rays `l1`,`l2` are equal if they have the same source and the other defining point of `l1` is contained in `l2`.

```C++
pgl::Ray l1(1,2,3,4), l2(2,3,1,2);
if (l1 != l2)
    std::cout << l1 << " != " << l2;
l2 = l2.opposite();
if (l1 == l2)
    std::cout << l1 << " == " << l2;
// Output: (1,2)--(3,4)-> != (2,3)--(1,2)->
//         (1,2)--(3,4)-> == (1,2)--(2,3)->
```

The defining points may be accessed as in an oriented segment and may be changed directly. The boundary of a ray is its source.

A ray `l` has methods such as:

- [`l.isDegenerate()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a68014d94d2e314877bf4a09aaf39ad39 "Returns whether the defining points coincide."): Returns `l[0] == l[1]`.
- [`l.isVertical()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a05e1dd210bf5c5c3ec92adfceb6d5fa5 "Returns whether the ray is vertical."): Returns `l[0].x() == l[1].x()`.
- [`l.isHorizontal()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#aa75a93acd72c880836337a5b2bae3b9f "Returns whether the ray is horizontal."): Returns `l[0].y() == l[1].y()`.
- [`l.opposite()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a749a6d7a25334d7d452c06c939a1ef24 "Returns the ray obtained by swapping the two stored defining points."): Returns the ray with source and target interchanged.
- [`l.slope()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a93ea798d1e9270ced1a7450346fda9b1 "Returns the slope of the supporting line."): Returns `(l[1].y()-l[0].y()) / (l[1].x()-l[0].x())`, possibly negative.
- [`l.parallel(t)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a31d03810caf016b8b5d4dcc8af4ad61d "Returns whether the given line is parallel to the ray."): Returns whether `l` and `t` have the same slope, but without using division. Here, `t` may be a segment, oriented segment, line, ray, or oriented line.
- [`l.halfplaneAbove()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a00aedab9d7a8015114914fd415645177 "Returns the half-plane geometrically above the supporting line."): Returns the half-plane defined by all points `p` that are above the line (larger y-coordinate). If the line is vertical, then it returns the half-plane with smaller x-coordinate. In other words, it returns the half-plane defined by all points `p` such that `pgl::OrientedSegment(l[0],l[1]).orientation(p) <= 0`, noticing that `l[0] < l[1]`.
- [`l.halfplaneBelow()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#aa8ccb101750ad8b384d3637e9ff74a76 "Returns the half-plane geometrically below the supporting line."): Returns the half-plane containing `l` and not [`halfplaneAbove`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a00aedab9d7a8015114914fd415645177 "Returns the half-plane geometrically above the supporting line.").
- [`l.orientation(p)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a0cb4c142550a83fb2a46205e3c566bd4 "Returns the orientation sign of a point with respect to the ray."): Given a point `p`, returns the orientation sign of `l[0],l[1],p`: null when they are collinear, negative when `l` sees `p` to its right, and positive when `l` sees `p` to its left.
- [`l.rightHalfplane()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a0a101de661262dab7e4bc1e48dffd724 "Returns the half-plane on the right of the ray direction."): Returns the half-plane defined by all points `p` such that `l.orientation(p) <= 0`.
- [`l.leftHalfplane()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a01083ffe650f8b9ffaa393f7574f6005 "Returns the half-plane on the left of the ray direction."): Returns the half-plane defined by all points `p` such that `l.orientation(p) >= 0`.
- [`l.yAtX(x)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#aca8c3ed33fcaafeb3a212cc37b1c51b7 "Returns the value of the y coordinate for a given x, if it exists."): Returns an `std::optional` with the value of the ray y coordinate at the given coordinate `x`.
- [`l.xAtY(y)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a1da16a6f7958862040effa675515dc07 "Returns the value of the x coordinate for a given y, if it exists."): Returns an `std::optional` with the value of the ray x coordinate at the given coordinate `y`.

It knows how to convert itself with an explicit cast to:
- `(pgl::Line) l` or [`l.asLine()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#ae47f5294685f87b606737e49cf1982f9 "Returns the supporting line without orientation."): Returns the line containing the ray.
- `(pgl::OrientedLine) l` or [`l.asOrientedLine()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a714c4d79f71316abc554f9b5f832b197 "Returns the oriented supporting line."): Returns the oriented line containing the ray and the same orientation.

- Other methods: [`area`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#accc8080e83502a2cdb1a60da7ad79fa6 "Returns the area of the ray."), [`begin`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#afeb98bee5538050e4db71ee4473529d4 "Returns an iterator to the source point."), [`boundaryContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#aa8fb539f32ed24a3b4ab72249754004c "Tests whether this shape's boundary contains the other shape (∂A ⊇ B)."), [`cbegin`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a8d6a17d7cb4bc074b891181c11cd62e1 "Returns an iterator to the source point."), [`cend`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a05eb2aeb0e782a643f3749d80d43f0fc "Returns an iterator past the target point."), [`collinear`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a2cd767b07ca2dd0f031dd7679c68d632 "Returns whether the given point is collinear with the ray."), [`contains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#ae0a3ac3654f116edc50c0b26e6838115 "Tests whether this shape contains the other shape (A ⊇ B)."), [`containsCollinear`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a3e26488ffd5e65ea1fbcf620ea155b64 "Returns whether the ray contains the given point that is collinear with the ray."), [`crosses`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a54b58104cdb1a3bef2fd04d3fcbb1876 "Tests whether the two shapes mutually separate each other (each disconnects the other)."), [`distanceL1`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#ae4f6c732976aca9b3a4d263f7f8a5664 "Returns the Manhattan (L1) distance to the given shape."), [`distanceLInf`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#ab9d136bb4dc01963dac4be687e2f477d "Returns the Chebyshev (LInf) distance to the given shape."), [`end`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a47b8e4fdc2b34894fc047e7ec50a53f9 "Returns an iterator past the target point."), [`get`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a22ddc33b71c1a6893470a05d65a87059 "Cyclic access: same as operator[] but index is taken modulo size(); negative indices wrap from the end."), [`index`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a3e9100b021385db17bd0f7e3c010830a "Returns the smallest index i with (*this)[i] == point, or -1 if no defining point equals point."), [`interiorContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a0af762abf9afa1c69da436b7e14afba6 "Tests whether this shape's interior contains the other shape (A∖∂A ⊇ B)."), [`interiorsIntersect`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a9dd77e5daffa10f9cf3980a79b84cfb3 "Tests whether the interiors of the two shapes intersect ((A∖∂A) ∩ (B∖∂B) ≠ ∅)."), [`intersection`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a64baaf5c8110942301642a63fbb8de25 "Returns the intersection of the two shapes (A ∩ B), empty when they are disjoint."), [`intersects`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#ac9a371022edbeb66f2c6b7dc1407cc98 "Tests whether this shape and the other shape intersect (A ∩ B ≠ ∅)."), [`label`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html#a5cb84ab9187e88bbaad09dfd09877157 "Returns the ray label."), ### Half-Plane

The class template [`Halfplane`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html "Closed half-plane defined by an oriented boundary line.") is stored as an oriented line, but represents a completely different geometric object that contains all points on its left half-plane. The boundary of the half-plane is the line that defines it. Two half-planes are equal if the corresponding oriented lines are equal:

```C++
pgl::Halfplane h1(1,2,3,4), h2(2,3,1,2);
if (h1 != h2)
    std::cout << h1 << " != " << h2;
// Output: ^-(1,2)--(3,4)-^ != ^-(2,3)--(1,2)-^

h2 = h2.opposite();
if (h1 == h2)
    std::cout << h1 << " == " << h2;
// Output: ^-(1,2)--(3,4)-^ == ^-(1,2)--(2,3)-^
```

The defining points may be accessed as in an oriented segment and may be changed directly.

A half-plane `h` has methods such as:

- [`h.isDegenerate()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#ae962754a791826b988b39273a9e0da7e "Returns whether the defining points coincide."): Returns `h[0] == h[1]`.
- [`h.isVertical()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#a86a553f8847f65c8e89fe1a612ffcfe3 "Returns whether the boundary line is vertical."): Returns `h[0].x() == h[1].x()`.
- [`h.isHorizontal()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#ae4b2bac2a079079c00870916a7c93b0f "Returns whether the boundary line is horizontal."): Returns `h[0].y() == h[1].y()`.
- [`h.opposite()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#a4bcdbe04bd9d5f266d6c1bdf0bc2ad9e "Returns the complementary half-plane with reversed boundary orientation."): Returns the half-plane with source and target interchanged.
- [`h.slope()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#aea8e2495979f8da401683ed157578993 "Returns the slope of the boundary line."): Returns `(h[1].y()-h[0].y()) / (h[1].x()-h[0].x())`, possibly negative.
- [`h.intersection(h2)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#a6ed9f14e8935b883ccf281944f6c9d06 "Returns the intersection of the two shapes (A ∩ B), empty when they are disjoint."): Intersecting with another half-plane returns a [`HalfplaneIntersection`](#halfplane-intersection) — exact and division-free, whether the result is a wedge, a strip, a nested half-plane, a line, or the empty set.

It knows how to convert itself with an explicit cast to:
- `(pgl::Line) l` or [`l.asLine()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#ade5884b09f45d35c46a09f9cf90c32b6 "Returns the boundary line without orientation."): Returns the line bounding the half-plane.
- `(pgl::OrientedLine) l` or [`l.asOrientedLine()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#ade8b1566d820ead8ec0d5865c2d46d3c "Returns the oriented boundary line."): Returns the oriented line bounding the half-plane.
- `(pgl::HalfplaneIntersection) h` or [`h.asHalfplaneIntersection()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#adbae0b62cd68d80f8654467a0fb9a229 "Returns the half-plane as a half-plane intersection."): Returns the half-plane as a one-constraint half-plane intersection.

- Other methods: [`begin`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#a47fc0d4cf739cb1ab75ca5593fd1ab2f "Returns an iterator to the source defining point."), [`boundaryContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#abdafb4b982b7a9ae9fdbc587c0bb8503 "Tests whether this shape's boundary contains the other shape (∂A ⊇ B)."), [`distanceL1`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#a680fd9297fdcaf1f06c60ec3f37e6a38 "Returns the Manhattan (L1) distance to the given shape."), the end."), [`index`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html#a27001adb22631bfa0a96debd380e5ee0 "Returns the smallest index i with (*this)[i] == point, or -1 if no defining point ### Triangle

The class template [`Triangle`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html "Closed triangle stored by three vertices.") is stored as three points, called vertices, which are kept in the following order. The first vertex is the smallest lexicographically and the other two vertices are ordered such that the triangle is oriented counterclockwise (positive orientation test). Two triangles are equal if they have the same vertices.

```C++
pgl::Triangle t(3,3,4,1,1,1);
std::cout << t << std::endl;
// Output: <(1,1)(4,1)(3,3)>
for(size_t i : {0,1,2}) std::cout << t[i] << ' ';
// Output: (1,1) (4,1) (3,3)
for(pgl::Point p : t) std::cout << p << ' ';
// Output: (1,1) (4,1) (3,3)
for(pgl::Segment s : t.edges()) std::cout << s << ' ';
// Output: (1,1)--(4,1) (3,3)--(4,1) (1,1)--(3,3)
for(pgl::OrientedSegment s : t.orientedEdges()) std::cout << s << ' ';
// Output: (1,1)->(4,1) (4,1)->(3,3) (3,3)->(1,1)
```

A triangle `t` has methods such as:

- [`t.isDegenerate()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#ab159614e587f6dc4a75789f98fb7a848 "Tests whether the three vertices are collinear."): Returns true if there are equal vertices or all vertices are collinear.
- `t.isPoint()` / `t.getIfPoint()`: Whether the triangle collapses to a single point (all defining points equal), and that point as a `std::optional<PointType>`.
- `t.isSegment()` / `t.getIfSegment()`: Whether the triangle collapses to a segment of positive length (defining points collinear but not all equal), and that segment as a `std::optional<Segment>`.
- `t.isUndefined()`: Always `false`: a degenerate triangle is always a point or a segment.
- [`t.centroid()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#af601a145a531947ed2b4475ab0102f78 "Returns the arithmetic centroid."): Returns the centroid.
- [`t.circumcircle()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#a0b9e50f4c10a634c4706884ff2894fb7 "Returns the circumcircle of the triangle."): Returns the circumcircle.
- [`t.isRectangle()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#a4cfb7d8fffa11293343c535bc4825e3f "Tests whether the triangle has a right angle."): Returns whether one angle is 90 degrees.
- [`t.isObtuse()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#a7848d637384e479a97395489bf9a61fb "Tests whether the triangle has an obtuse angle."): Returns whether one angle is greater than 90 degrees.
- [`t.isIsosceles()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#a3cc30d928e1cb21c99eae9765f38e3d4 "Tests whether two sides have the same length."): Returns whether two sides have the same length.

It knows how to convert itself with an explicit cast to:
- `(pgl::Polygon) t` or [`t.asPolygon()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#aaa078dd16798d8d58bb3cb46f64aba3b "Returns the triangle as a simple polygon."): Returns the polygon representation of the triangle.
- `(pgl::Convex) t` or [`t.asConvex()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#ab01b4c98a0859edd28f1961012ff1447 "Returns the triangle as a convex polygon."): Returns the convex polygon representation of the triangle.

- Other methods: [`a`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#a49e6b3ba0db01d986087b842b54d9131 "Returns the first vertex."), [`area`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#a7d9d7443fe0e474c2a40cc81eeda5aa3 "Returns the non-negative area of the triangle."), [`b`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#a2940bbceda1e86124340be582ae78283 "Returns the second vertex."), [`bbox`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#a9b3e0556b074ae291d5db07c827463c9 "Returns the axis-aligned bounding box of the vertices."), [`begin`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#a3bf2365cdca283316f0deb0f3d0377fa "Returns an iterator to the first vertex."), [`boundaryContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#a7b7065114ca1f45e385600fb5fcb6530 "Tests whether this shape's boundary contains the other shape (∂A ⊇ B)."), [`c`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#ae3d2e34092959e6f78dbd305910f34ac "Returns the third vertex."), [`cbegin`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html#a642ff0f840b22d623caf3c1f1562a102 "Returns an iterator to the first vertex."), ### Rectangle

The class template [`Rectangle`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html "Axis-aligned rectangle stored by minimum and maximum corners.") represents an axis-aligned rectangle. While it is stored internally as only two vertices (minimum and maximum x and y coordinates), it behaves as a polygon with four vertices. It can be constructed for any number of points in a container and will construct the bounding box rectangle. If only two points are given, the container is optional. If the two points are respectively the minimum x and y and the maximum x and y, then an optional argument set to true avoids the bounding box calculation.

```C++
pgl::Rectangle r({{1,3},{2,4},{3,1},{5,4},{2,3}});
// Same as pgl::Rectangle r({1,1},{5,4}) or pgl::Rectangle r({1,4},{5,1});
std::cout << r << std::endl;
// Output: [(1,1),(5,4)]
std::cout << r.min() << ' ' << r.max() << std::endl;
// Output: (1,1) (5,4)
for(size_t i : {0,1,2,3}) std::cout << r[i] << ' ';
// Output: (1,1) (5,1) (5,4) (1,4)
for(pgl::Point p : r) std::cout << p << ' ';
// Output: (1,1) (5,1) (5,4) (1,4)
for(pgl::Segment s : r.edges()) std::cout << s << ' ';
// Output: (1,1)--(5,1) (5,1)--(5,4) (1,4)--(5,4) (1,1)--(1,4)
for(pgl::OrientedSegment s : r.orientedEdges()) std::cout << s << ' ';
// Output: (1,1)->(5,1) (5,1)->(5,4) (5,4)->(1,4) (1,4)->(1,1)
```

A rectangle `r` has methods such as:

- [`r.isDegenerate()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#a380388f13b2cd9a5ad8f4f806e31be84 "Returns whether the rectangle has empty interior."): Returns true if the rectangle has null area.
- `r.isPoint()` / `r.getIfPoint()`: Whether the rectangle collapses to a single point (all defining points equal), and that point as a `std::optional<PointType>`.
- `r.isSegment()` / `r.getIfSegment()`: Whether the rectangle collapses to a segment of positive length (defining points collinear but not all equal), and that segment as a `std::optional<Segment>`.
- `r.isUndefined()`: Always `false`: a degenerate rectangle is always a point or a segment.
- [`r.centroid()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#a553634d0d486ae85ec22c6a036855f0e "Returns the centroid of the rectangle."): Returns the centroid.
- [`r.circumcircle()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#a96f720c89b9116686118e6085374a7ab "Returns the circumcircle of the rectangle."): Returns the circumcircle.
- [`r.insert(s)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#a02c20ec22014c7dbd29d3b2489527b59 "Enlarges the rectangle so that it contains the given point."): Enlarges the rectangle in order to contain a finite shape `s`. The shape must expose [`bbox()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#ae88798364c0bf20cd4d14c259fa33dcd "Returns the bounding box of the rectangle.").
- [`r.insert(points)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#a02c20ec22014c7dbd29d3b2489527b59 "Enlarges the rectangle so that it contains the given point."): Enlarges the rectangle in order to contain every point in the input range.

It knows how to convert itself with an explicit cast to:
- `(pgl::Polygon) r` or [`r.asPolygon()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#af850885808c25c90aaa04506cc1f1537 "Returns the rectangle as a simple polygon."): Returns the polygon representation of the rectangle.
- `(pgl::Convex) r` or [`r.asConvex()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#a9c20bb08763579e119ea85d68a907ab3 "Returns the rectangle as a convex polygon."): Returns the convex polygon representation of the rectangle.

- Other methods: [`area`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#af8e8f46b3461676c7e40a14b6c4ff86c "Returns the rectangle area."), [`begin`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#aae952c36436a16afb5bff4f8929cceb8 "Returns an iterator to the minimum corner."), [`boundaryAt`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#a3565ced82f92e91b79f1aa9be6df761d), [`boundaryContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#ab6df84e85e6d33ff9212783b62dd98c9 "Tests whether this shape's boundary contains the other shape (∂A ⊇ B)."), [`cbegin`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#a118731b6ddcbd04097b0cf76136958d2 "Returns an iterator to the minimum corner."), [`cend`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#ad52301729cc8376c6c9dbbcebcb769d4 "Returns an iterator past the last corner."), [`center`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#a7d2e656f0e136e0978ac785096178df7 "Returns the center of the rectangle."), [`contains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#ad43d090a0253e4f66e8f73f6288e0fcc "Tests whether this shape contains the other shape (A ⊇ B)."), [`crosses`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#ade1e0e5f80655722aa51c65e37e08e82 "Tests whether the two shapes mutually separate each other (each disconnects the other)."), [`diameter`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#af310096576e07b538fc1b3eb2ee2f74e "Returns a segment defining a diameter."), [`distanceL1`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#ac07f544b22bb10991bf113774e8564e5 "Returns the Manhattan (L1) distance to the given shape."), [`distanceLInf`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#a8935a3e41149260d3dca1fac6cf69939 "Returns the Chebyshev (LInf) distance to the given shape."), [`edges`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#a7017ee8e082661250fdcdf9948033f71 "Returns the four edges as unordered segments."),
