### Disk

The class template [`Disk`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html "Closed Euclidean disk stored by boundary points plus optional disk label.") represents a circle with its interior. Disks are stored internally as three boundary points, in the same way as a [`Triangle`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html "Closed triangle stored by three vertices."). This choice may be surprising, as the standard representation for disks is a center point and a radius. The main motivation is that the circumcircle of a triangle may be represented exactly for integers. Nevertheless, the constructor accepts both forms:

```C++
pgl::Disk d1({1,1}, {2,5}, {4,3}); // Disk from 3 points
pgl::Disk d2({2,3}, 4);            // Disk from a point and a radius
std::cout << d2 << std::endl;
// Output: Disk((-2,3)(6,3)(2,7))  // Output always uses 3 points
```

Disk does not have the [`intersection`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#aa9af5f0f6a0a4d6d6139d6c3697a3abd "Returns the intersection of the two shapes (A ∩ B), empty when they are disjoint.") method and cannot be scaled on a single axis. A disk `d` has methods such as:

- [`d.isDegenerate()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a19e32c8b7656bfe02982c8a40ea0ff57 "Returns whether the three boundary points are collinear."): Returns true if the points are collinear or equal.
- `d.isPoint()` / `d.getIfPoint()`: Whether the disk collapses to a single point (all defining points equal), and that point as a `std::optional<PointType>`.
- `d.isUndefined()`: True if the boundary points are collinear but not all equal, so they do not determine a circle (three distinct collinear points have no circle through them; two distinct ones have infinitely many). A disk is never a segment, so this and `isPoint` cover every degenerate disk.
- [`d.radius()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a69765084e501902a81583032fdb7c816 "Returns the center in this disk's exact coordinate type."): Returns the radius length.
- [`d.squaredRadius()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a0027c4ab0d85b7ff3e5b0d5b42b1745f "Returns the squared radius in an explicitly chosen result type."): Returns the squared radius.
- [`d.center()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a01f21dd5b971164474843df4ad71bfc1 "Returns the center (circumcenter of the three boundary points) in an explicitly chosen coordinate type."): Returns the center point.
- [`d.diameter()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#ad7c86ba7778cf349bcb6c653e0470ba5 "Returns a segment defining a diameter of the disk."): As always returns a diameter [`Segment`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html "Unoriented closed segment between two endpoints plus optional segment label."), but for disks the segment is always horizontal.

- Other methods: [`a`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#aab80a3c4810748f39fdc8c7e020eb232 "Returns the first boundary point (lexicographically smallest)."), [`area`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#aa4f2837f14655fd73098513f769e41da "Returns the squared radius in this disk's exact result type."), [`b`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#aeb247fff089ba4d4463418f84f0f9e3d "Returns the second boundary point in canonical order."), [`bbox`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a82543add1cf92f0b0116fd798ddecced "Returns an axis-aligned bounding box in the coordinate type."), [`begin`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#aaec2b4934b1b89530f0ea126a080f4ef "Returns an iterator to the first boundary point."), [`boundaryContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a190ee43500b52f235991dc9bc3095180 "Tests whether this shape's boundary contains the other shape (∂A ⊇ B)."), [`c`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a20d60679286b84a64063b648c860430e "Returns the third boundary point in canonical order."), [`cbegin`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#ad39c1b05f308f292112590c388575a36 "Returns a const iterator to the first boundary point."), [`cend`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a5c10cddfceeff5a571f4b892ba2e11ad "Returns a const iterator past the last boundary point."), [`contains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a40627a86d5eb68250e7daba7a1ca36db "Tests whether this shape contains the other shape (A ⊇ B)."), [`crosses`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a3afd10decc72c4a11747024bee9c635b "Tests whether the two shapes mutually separate each other (each disconnects the other)."), [`distanceL1`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a569e24bcd39f95c5901e956fd1ea70d7 "Returns the Manhattan (L1) distance from this disk to a point."), [`distanceLInf`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a0c438698a94776de27b7738d325066f3 "Returns the Chebyshev (LInf) distance from this disk to a point."), [`end`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a4245360f58a74d7969b4176627797e8f "Returns an iterator past the last boundary point."), [`fbox`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#aff00091cb8c1a2afd46f55e31fb4d38f "Returns a floating-point bounding box of the disk."), [`get`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#afd129f4cb8111846762e2b4ca06db7c1 "Cyclic access: same as operator[] but index is taken modulo size(); negative indices wrap from the end."), [`index`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#ab6e63f207efba3178e1a665c70f60cd0 "Returns the smallest index i with (*this)[i] == point, or -1 if no boundary point equals point."), [`interiorContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a5c43e8fa09ae818be5fa70d2f18c9474 "Tests whether this shape's interior contains the other shape (A∖∂A ⊇ B)."), [`interiorsIntersect`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a7932e3c1de11bf765ca378582df887e6 "Tests whether the interiors of the two shapes intersect ((A∖∂A) ∩ (B∖∂B) ≠ ∅)."), [`intersects`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a04a545293c1496c34b4bf601a124eff6 "Tests whether this shape and the other shape intersect (A ∩ B ≠ ∅)."), [`label`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#af5f96f809e41fead49b9cafa8de11938 "Returns the disk label."), [`pointInside`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a78477a3bcf69c6f36cff63e5a65442e3 "Returns the midpoint of the first two boundary points."), [`pointInsideInteriorContainedIn`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a215ae2e3722c294b6c66b80b50ab59b5 "Tests whether some point strictly inside this disk lies in the strict interior of shape."), [`rotate90`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a8d38ec21e40e6459475c0006646d352b "Rotates the disk by 90k degrees around the origin in place."), [`rotated90`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a07288ad0eb805ea8a8e6a67d5b8e497c "Returns the disk rotated by 90k degrees around the origin."), [`separates`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#ae384cc53e8754c9618a33a6a0d58945c "Tests whether removing this shape disconnects the other shape (B∖A is disconnected)."), [`size`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a9276993379a1097fb9a2c3464faf66ed "Returns the number of stored boundary points (always 3)."), [`squaredDistance`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a714b6843c9d986d4da9776c33528cf47 "Returns the squared Euclidean distance from this disk to a point.").


### Monotone Chain

The class template [`MonotoneChain`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html "Weakly x-monotone polyline stored by lexicographically sorted vertices.") represents an x-monotone polyline: a polyline whose vertices are strictly increasing in the lexicographic order (smaller x first, breaking ties by smaller y). A chain with $n$ vertices has $n-1$ edges and no closing edge, so it is an open curve and is automatically simple. Its boundary is its two extreme vertices and its interior is everything else.

A chain may be constructed from any container of points, which will be sorted automatically and duplicates removed. The input is treated as a point set, not as a pre-linked chain, so any permutation of the same points yields the same chain. If the points are already sorted and unique, a second parameter true can be given to avoid sorting the points again.

We use the term above to refer to larger y coordinates and below to refer to smaller y coordinates. A chain `P` with $n$ vertices has methods such as:

- [`P.isDegenerate()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#a7eb13f8c59d5d0798e3e15bfca4739a6 "Checks if the chain is degenerate (fewer than two vertices, so it has no edge)."): Returns true if the chain has fewer than two vertices, and hence no edge.
- `P.isPoint()` / `P.getIfPoint()`: Whether the chain collapses to a single point (all defining points equal), and that point as a `std::optional<PointType>`.
- `P.isSegment()` / `P.getIfSegment()`: Whether the chain collapses to a segment of positive length (defining points collinear but not all equal), and that segment as a `std::optional<Segment>`.
- `P.isUndefined()`: True only for an empty chain, which has no vertex.
- [`P.isStrictlyMonotone()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#ad6166871233a0625fdd3ce18f00b2f5c "Tests whether the chain is strictly x-monotone."): Returns true if no two vertices share an x-coordinate, so the chain is the graph of a function of x. Takes $O(n)$ time.
- [`P.insert(p)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#a5cd1ec77da22ad3d957149d51e3d8a05 "Extends the chain to contain the given point as a vertex."): Extends the chain in order to contain another point `p` as a vertex.
- [`P.insert(points)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#a5cd1ec77da22ad3d957149d51e3d8a05 "Extends the chain to contain the given point as a vertex."): Extends the chain in order to contain all the given points as vertices.
- [`P.indexAtX(x)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#a393d5b3cc782e79a7036ee3beb72257f "Locates the vertex or edge of the chain at a given x-coordinate."): Returns an `std::optional<size_t>` that is engaged if the chain contains a point of x-coordinate `x`. The returned value is the smallest index `i` such that `P[i].x() == x`, or the unique `i` with `P[i].x() < x < P[i+1].x()`. Takes $O(\log n)$ time.
- [`P.yAtX(x)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#a9f8a70bee1528bc6809112b9916fae85 "Evaluates the y-coordinate of the chain at a given x-coordinate."): Returns an `std::optional` with the value of the y coordinate at the given coordinate `x` (at a vertical edge, the y of the edge's bottom vertex). Takes $O(\log n)$ time. **Warning:** interpolation divides, so request a floating-point or [`Rational`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/classpgl_1_1Rational.html "Exact rational number class template.") result type for an accurate value.
- [`P.isBelow(p)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#a4bc4bcb2b9210d4d4d320556798ee343 "Tests whether the chain passes weakly below a point."): Returns an `std::optional<size_t>` that is engaged if a ray shot down from `p` intersects `P`; the value is the index [`indexAtX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#a393d5b3cc782e79a7036ee3beb72257f "Locates the vertex or edge of the chain at a given x-coordinate.") returns for `p.x()`. Takes $O(\log n)$ time, exactly.
- [`P.isAbove(p)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#a9eaf22f78515682edac8b3b1590615f1 "Tests whether the chain passes weakly above a point."): The same for a ray shot up from `p`. Note that [`isBelow`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#a4bc4bcb2b9210d4d4d320556798ee343 "Tests whether the chain passes weakly below a point.") and [`isAbove`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#a9eaf22f78515682edac8b3b1590615f1 "Tests whether the chain passes weakly above a point.") are not complementary: both are engaged when `p` lies on the chain.
- [`P.length()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#aeeb80f55a73ab440b6e119e02827ee74 "Computes the Euclidean length of the chain (the sum of its edge lengths)."), [`P.lengthL1()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#afca60f5968fff4ff8e6a1d8c38aeeaa3 "Computes the Manhattan (L1) length of the chain."), [`P.lengthLInf()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#a6b0f1704432d4fff888a8cd935a499f9 "Computes the Chebyshev (LInf) length of the chain."): Return the Euclidean, Manhattan, and Chebyshev lengths of the chain.

The monotone structure speeds up several predicates and constructions:

- [`P.contains(s)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#a5ca0039a7ceff302c988f0ee2fca3226 "Tests whether this shape contains the other shape (A ⊇ B).") takes $O(\log n)$ time if `s` is a point, and $O(\log n + k)$ if `s` is a segment whose x-range spans $k$ vertices (a chain contains a segment exactly when the segment is a straight sub-path of the chain).
- [`P.intersects(s)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#a3ff7a150d0eff57b3538d5375c299cec "Tests whether this shape and the other shape intersect (A ∩ B ≠ ∅).") takes $O(\log n + k)$ time for a segment overlapping $k$ edges of the chain.
- [`P.intersects(P2)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#a3ff7a150d0eff57b3538d5375c299cec "Tests whether this shape and the other shape intersect (A ∩ B ≠ ∅).") and [`P.intersection(P2)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#acf0c387012c4d665ac17e0c3fecec81b "Returns the intersection of the two shapes (A ∩ B), empty when they are disjoint.") take $O(n+m)$ time if `P2` is a chain with $m$ vertices, via a merge sweep over the two sorted vertex sequences. [`P.intersection(s)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#acf0c387012c4d665ac17e0c3fecec81b "Returns the intersection of the two shapes (A ∩ B), empty when they are disjoint.") returns an `std::vector` of points and segments sorted by the lexicographic order, with collinear overlaps coalesced; the same form is returned for segments, lines, rays, halfplanes, rectangles, triangles, and convex polygons.
- [`P.edgesCross(P2)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#a45e24dfac70758449ba7139d2b04c956 "Tests whether the two chains have edges that cross."): Returns true if `P` has a point strictly above `P2` and a point strictly below it, i.e. every sufficiently small perturbation of the vertices of `P` and `P2` still yields intersecting chains. Unlike [`P.crosses(P2)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html#ac70944fe81641ed674b8a30e44f246c1 "Tests whether the two shapes mutually separate each other (each disconnects the other)."), a touch that does not swap sides never counts. The x-extents of `P` and `P2` must overlap in more than a single point, or the result is false outright — a shared x that is only one chain's own extreme vertex (e.g. a chain that is a single vertical edge) is not robust to perturbation. Takes $O(n \log m + m \log n)$ time if `P2` has $m$ vertices.


### Polyline

The class template [`Polyline`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html "Open polygonal chain stored in traversal order; may self-intersect.") represents a polyline, also called a polygonal chain, polygonal curve, polygonal path, or piecewise linear curve. Unlike [`MonotoneChain`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html "Weakly x-monotone polyline stored by lexicographically sorted vertices."), the order of the vertices matter and the polyline is allowed to self-intersect. A polyline with $n$ vertices has $n-1$ edges and no closing edge. Its boundary is its two extreme vertices and its interior is everything else.

A polyline can be constructed from any container of points, or from a flat list of coordinates. The only normalization is the direction: the constructor the smallest extreme at index 0. If the sequence is already in canonical direction, a second parameter true skips the check.

A polyline `P` with $n$ vertices has methods such as:

- [`P.isDegenerate()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a3c7ecb999d35c0430c7ec57468f9d9d1 "Checks if the polyline is degenerate (all vertices are equal, so it covers at most a single point)."): Returns true if all vertices are equal (in particular for an empty or single-vertex polyline).
- `P.isPoint()` / `P.getIfPoint()`: Whether the polyline collapses to a single point (all defining points equal), and that point as a `std::optional<PointType>`.
- `P.isSegment()` / `P.getIfSegment()`: Whether the polyline collapses to a segment of positive length (defining points collinear but not all equal), and that segment as a `std::optional<Segment>`.
- `P.isUndefined()`: True only for an empty polyline, which has no vertex.
- [`P.isSimple()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a109775a30d3c17acb2b610e5139a17ee "Tests whether the polyline is simple (it does not touch or cross itself)."): Returns true if the edges only intersect at the shared endpoints of consecutive edges. In an open chain the first and last edges are not consecutive, so a closed polyline (first vertex equal to the last) is not simple. Takes $O(n \log n)$ time for exact coordinate types (and $O(n^2)$ for floating point).
- [`P.length()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a9317b71453005525d3e6d20309478119 "Computes the Euclidean length of the polyline (the sum of its edge lengths)."), [`P.lengthL1()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#aa07788cbfbca38496e4920d9a6cbc827 "Computes the Manhattan (L1) length of the polyline."), [`P.lengthLInf()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a6a8eb393f3ee27522fc9de7d3601475a "Computes the Chebyshev (LInf) length of the polyline."): Return the Euclidean, Manhattan, and Chebyshev lengths of the polyline. A self-overlapping polyline counts every traversal of a repeated part.

Since a self-intersecting polyline has no monotone structure to exploit, the predicates scan the edges: they take $O(n)$ time against a point or a segment (segment containment takes $O(n \log n)$, as a segment may be covered by several non-consecutive collinear edges) and $O(nm)$ time against another polyline with $m$ vertices. The cut predicates use set semantics: [`separates`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a2732dc06ae36d3f65d3f6b554bd02e76 "Tests whether removing this shape disconnects the other shape (B∖A is disconnected).") joins the surviving pieces through self-crossings and revisited vertices, so removing one point never disconnects a closed polyline. Against a two-dimensional region (halfplane, rectangle, triangle, disk, convex, or polygon), [`separates`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a2732dc06ae36d3f65d3f6b554bd02e76 "Tests whether removing this shape disconnects the other shape (B∖A is disconnected).") searches the polyline's self-intersection arrangement (built exactly, in $O(n^2)$ pairwise edge intersections) for a cycle through the region's interior, so it also detects a pocket sealed by a loop that never touches the region's boundary.

[`P.intersection(s)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a615fbb18098d3774821bd2b932203c02 "Returns the intersection of the two shapes (A ∩ B), empty when they are disjoint.") returns an `std::vector` of points and segments sorted by the lexicographic order, in the same form as [`MonotoneChain`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1MonotoneChain.html "Weakly x-monotone polyline stored by lexicographically sorted vertices."), for segments, lines, rays, halfplanes, rectangles, triangles, convex polygons, monotone chains, and other polylines. Pieces are maximal even though a self-intersecting polyline may report them out of traversal order: collinear touching overlaps are merged (also when they come from non-consecutive edges) and points covered by a reported segment are dropped. It takes $O(n)$ segment intersections against a single shape and $O(nm)$ against a chain or polyline with $m$ vertices, plus a coalescing step quadratic in the number of pieces.

The predicates, distances, and [`intersection`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a615fbb18098d3774821bd2b932203c02 "Returns the intersection of the two shapes (A ∩ B), empty when they are disjoint.") are implemented against every shape that supports them; membership in [`Shape`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Shape.html "Runtime variant wrapper over the supported primitive shapes.") is planned.

- Other methods: [`bbox`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a0f6e26a628acedb87aada9fb34159c89 "Computes the bounding box of the polyline."), [`begin`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a7fc2ac2f29533e0e0ae85817c58041d4 "Returns a constant iterator to the first vertex."), [`boundaryContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#aa4d5c3d3cf700d00e22da815a4a426de "Tests whether this shape's boundary contains the other shape (∂A ⊇ B)."), [`cbegin`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#afd424fe9a2ac204ba664ef9dc982b448 "Returns a constant iterator to the first vertex."), [`cend`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#aa6d8896dd6fa04c273cd62bab800abaf "Returns a constant iterator past the last vertex."), [`contains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a8d0533b6ff156b2ee349a702b9d6c403 "Tests whether this shape contains the other shape (A ⊇ B)."), [`crosses`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#aebb135e0f5c547fe7f5706cdca8d93a1 "Tests whether the two shapes mutually separate each other (each disconnects the other)."), [`diameter`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#ad58e4c2e63c54448fa1cda4f0025f67f "Returns a segment realizing the diameter (the farthest vertex pair)."), [`distanceL1`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#ad11916b51735271db1644e7aed3269bc "Returns the Manhattan (L1) distance to the given shape."), [`distanceLInf`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a06f8935547ebe27b5d33c270c5f80c94 "Returns the Chebyshev (LInf) distance to the given shape."), [`edges`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#ab6a4485c970a76a715ac8cdb53a3c3ae "Returns the edges of the polyline."), [`edgesBegin`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a7d2cd58632acab7505fb2b7e11b6be3a "Returns an iterator to the first unoriented edge."), [`edgesEnd`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a4ca470b37e64034714e7d2d666d13133 "Returns an iterator past the last unoriented edge."), [`edgesView`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#affa6b9b86d306b89a7616d32c9c56717 "Returns a lazy view over the edges, materializing each Segment on the fly instead of allocating a vector."), [`empty`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#aaf09aab7866b1c11308bfc2ec48ebd41 "Checks whether the polyline has no vertex."), [`end`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a8b3116bebd12969125b0a2398dd80edc "Returns a constant iterator past the last vertex."), [`fbox`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a596ba11db2c2a5af6634516267e672a5 "Computes the floating-point bounding box of the polyline."), [`flip`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a74569364f1e03939781c61e97c9ce26d "Flips oldEdge to newEdge in place."), [`flippable`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a43a572d69cc82cb20c464de87c870709 "Tests whether oldEdge can be flipped to newEdge."), [`flipped`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a3c6a8621dfda1022b020b0484aa63892 "Returns the polyline with oldEdge flipped to newEdge."), [`get`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a88d8370a57e89159083f7f67218d1a9c "Accesses a vertex by index modulo the vertex count."), [`index`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a6127f4d2249823ad30b95e1ace380c8e "Returns the smallest index i with (*this)[i] == point, or -1 if point is not a vertex."), [`interiorContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#afb0ee9bea14097783cb307615f483a34 "Tests whether this shape's interior contains the other shape (A∖∂A ⊇ B)."), [`interiorsIntersect`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a95b79e8265b13ad3b5568ae0f6785684 "Tests whether the interiors of the shapes intersect (A° ∩ B° ≠ ∅)."), [`intersects`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a9e4508c932fc0bb5f9bc054e9b0634a2 "Tests whether this shape and the other shape intersect (A ∩ B ≠ ∅)."), [`label`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a82a3ad176d39b8b6972a28562c6adc7a "Returns the polyline label."), [`orientedEdges`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a7e28c494881b86066d5a0ae1452ad341 "Returns the oriented edges of the polyline, each directed from vertex i to vertex i + 1 in traversal order."), [`orientedEdgesBegin`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a018ceb32a40d6cd9f509a1ca501494b7 "Returns an iterator to the first oriented edge."), [`orientedEdgesEnd`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a6f8b84820ce4f45d8ca3dd083dae8dc9 "Returns an iterator past the last oriented edge."), [`orientedEdgesView`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a98ff96bd90ece85b50b6ee04b67bdafa "Lazy view counterpart of orientedEdges(); see edgesView()."), [`pointInside`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#aac18f99afe9c98f8da82d38f804cbe70 "Returns a point inside the polyline."), [`polygonIntersection`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a6ee5107043443346b6dcb5ed7574f346 "Returns the intersection with a polygon (A ∩ B), a sequence of points and segments sorted by lexicographic order."), [`rotate90`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a2cc3afdb5d34613bb633ef042ba1ec3e "Rotates the polyline by 90k degrees around the origin in place."), [`rotated90`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a88d76f3590d6368f2dbdacc6d6cfb318 "Returns the polyline rotated by 90k degrees around the origin."), [`scaleDownX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a76e813756a16eb766d514001346d9f20 "Divides the polyline's x-coordinates by a divisor in place."), [`scaleDownY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#aea07da15eeea6a9c900fb42a6cb9fde8 "Divides the polyline's y-coordinates by a divisor in place."), [`scaleUpX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a5f576177d6edeecc1013c710ebe96629 "Multiplies the polyline's x-coordinates by a factor in place."), [`scaleUpY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a108c436aad4d816e3a7dde7e3b64dc93 "Multiplies the polyline's y-coordinates by a factor in place."), [`scaledDownX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a965b73578bf22e03bbfba04fe7fe199b "Returns the polyline with its x-coordinates divided by a divisor."), [`scaledDownY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#ae1afefbc2850a63d6976ffa11397ba05 "Returns the polyline with its y-coordinates divided by a divisor."), [`scaledUpX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a8dc5b5f26dc605638e7850c9b2973a68 "Returns the polyline with its x-coordinates multiplied by a factor."), [`scaledUpY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#adeac1628802f5cbb817ab0bcceb1f6c9 "Returns the polyline with its y-coordinates multiplied by a factor."), [`size`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#ad621eb6c07a174dba0275b3ac9cdb982 "Returns the number of vertices in the polyline."), [`squaredDistance`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#aed0a0c982b916cd1bb2930192f08cfdc "Returns the squared Euclidean distance to the given shape."), [`vertices`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polyline.html#a5521a7f3ebcda9cfc23f918477728cd2 "Returns the vertices of the polyline (translation applied).").


### Polygon

The class template [`Polygon`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html "Closed simple polygon stored by its vertices.") represents a simple polygon. It can be constructed for any number of points in a container that must be given in the order they appear on the polygon. The vertices are accessed in counterclockwise order starting from the minimum vertex (minimum x, breaking ties by minimum y).

A polygon `P` has methods such as:

- [`P.isDegenerate()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#af1d297b10aee661458543c2f5fa00645 "Checks if the polygon is degenerate (has zero area)."): Returns true if the polygon has null area.
- `P.isPoint()` / `P.getIfPoint()`: Whether the polygon collapses to a single point (all defining points equal), and that point as a `std::optional<PointType>`.
- `P.isSegment()` / `P.getIfSegment()`: Whether the polygon collapses to a segment of positive length (defining points collinear but not all equal), and that segment as a `std::optional<Segment>`.
- `P.isUndefined()`: True if the polygon is degenerate yet covers more than a segment: an empty polygon, or one whose zero area comes from a self-overlapping boundary rather than from collinear vertices.
- [`P.isSimple()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a5e182cf106f6be5ed3fb49c07daf80c6 "Tests whether the polygon is simple (its boundary does not touch or cross itself)."): Returns true if the edges only intersect at the endpoints of consecutive edges. Takes $O(n \log n)$ time for $n$ edges.
- [`P.isConvex()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a4fd2d656a5e668f8679db56d2d7d5257 "Tests whether the polygon is convex."): Returns true if the polygon is convex, possibly with vertices subdividing convex hull edges. Takes $O(n)$ time.
- [`P.untangle()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#ab85503d835bc7afa41dedd1140caa1dc "Makes the polygon simple in place by uncrossing its boundary."): Makes the polygon simple in place. Edges that cross are flipped and when a flip is blocked by collinearity (collinear vertices) the offending vertex is removed. On return [`P.isSimple()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a5e182cf106f6be5ed3fb49c07daf80c6 "Tests whether the polygon is simple (its boundary does not touch or cross itself).") holds. Worst-case complexity is high.

- Other methods: [`area`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#ad4765e3f6390f06a59f25fef4fcb219f "Computes the area of the polygon."), [`bbox`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a1ff93373098baf44e68ba00bb02565d6 "Computes the bounding box of the polygon."), [`begin`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a9411cd18c37f8f81740ce5ad1cf834aa "Returns a constant iterator to the first vertex."), [`boundariesIntersect`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a9121e0e14826affd49d8cb7bf89dde1a "Tests whether the two polygon boundaries share at least one point (∂A ∩ ∂B ≠ ∅)."), [`boundariesStrongCross`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a9f4343abe5859ec50774053edbfd7bc5 "Tests whether the two polygon boundaries have mononotone chains that strong cross."), [`boundaryContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a27e7ae09110e8b6d3a0c3b18885d9302 "Tests whether this shape's boundary contains the other shape (∂A ⊇ [`verticesCentroid`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#af868c2942503c2bafaf89f64af1fe463 "Computes the centroid of the vertex set (the average of the vertices).").


### Convex

The class template [`Convex`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html "Closed convex polygon stored by its vertices.") represents a convex polygon. It can be constructed for any number of points in a container and will construct the convex hull. The vertices are stored in counterclockwise order starting from the minimum vertex (minimum x, breaking ties by minimum y). If the container already has the vertices in order, a second constructor parameter can be set to true to avoid computing the convex hull.

A convex polygon `c` has methods such as:

- [`c.isDegenerate()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a460c2cbe73f75e60d0153be2acb1662d "Checks if the convex polygon is degenerate (has zero area)."): Returns true if the convex polygon has null area.
- `c.isPoint()` / `c.getIfPoint()`: Whether the polygon collapses to a single point (all defining points equal), and that point as a `std::optional<PointType>`.
- `c.isSegment()` / `c.getIfSegment()`: Whether the polygon collapses to a segment of positive length (defining points collinear but not all equal), and that segment as a `std::optional<Segment>`.
- `c.isUndefined()`: True only for an empty convex polygon, which has no vertex.
- [`c.centroid()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#ad4000c6649317b13e2695a209d9102e8 "Computes the centroid of the convex polygon."): Returns the centroid.
- [`c.insert(s)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a573355b49f7a15907cd3dec3ac3e8624 "Enlarges the convex polygon so that it contains the given point."): Enlarges the convex polygon in order to contain a finite shape `s`. The shape must expose its vertices.
- [`c.insert(points)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a573355b49f7a15907cd3dec3ac3e8624 "Enlarges the convex polygon so that it contains the given point."): Enlarges the convex polygon in order to contain every point in the input range.
- [`c.upperHull()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a34084131cdb827a1cf75bdc56e0018de "Returns the upper hull: the boundary chain running from the lexicographically smallest vertex to the lexicographically largest one, clockwise (above the polygon)."): Returns the upper monotone chain.
- [`c.lowerHull()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a08a0bfcddc1960c1f8ca2f5906730fdf "Returns the lower hull: the boundary chain running from the lexicographically smallest vertex to the lexicographically largest one, counterclockwise (below the polygon)."): Returns the lower monotone chain.

It knows how to convert itself with an explicit cast to:
- `(pgl::Polygon) c` or [`c.asPolygon()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#ab08ccc569a03b8056db708b1f4e26e2f "Returns the convex polygon as a simple polygon."): Returns the polygon representation of the convex polygon.

If the convex polygon `c` has $n$ vertices, then:

- [`c.diameter()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#ad44a3c0e434930328c723fcae6c4c7c9 "Returns a segment realizing the diameter (the farthest vertex pair).") takes $O(n)$ time.
- [`c.intersects(s)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a0717c549a9515d6c65cf68e620361239 "Tests whether this shape and the other shape intersect (A ∩ B ≠ ∅).") takes $O(\log n)$ time if `s` is a shape with $O(1)$ vertices (not including Disk).
- [`s.intersects(c)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a0717c549a9515d6c65cf68e620361239 "Tests whether this shape and the other shape intersect (A ∩ B ≠ ∅).") takes $O(\log n)$ time if `s` is a shape with $O(1)$ vertices (not including Disk).
- [`c.intersects(c2)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a0717c549a9515d6c65cf68e620361239 "Tests whether this shape and the other shape intersect (A ∩ B ≠ ∅).") takes $O(\min(n+m) \log(n+m))$ time if `c2` is a convex polygon with $m$ vertices.
- Other predicates take the same time as [`intersects`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a0717c549a9515d6c65cf68e620361239 "Tests whether this shape and the other shape intersect (A ∩ B ≠ ∅).").
- [`c.intersection(c2)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a56ad5f95e9f45d5f00af38f50470c7ea "Returns the intersection of the two shapes (A ∩ B), empty when they are disjoint.") takes $O((n+m) log (n+m))$ time if `c2` is a convex polygon with $m$ vertices.

- Other methods: [`antipodalPairs`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#ad8daeec7e468c1b3a12be81c96752f95 "Returns every antipodal vertex-index pair, via rotating calipers."), [`area`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a75a11db621ea33f8ea69f4946de7034e "Computes the area of the convex polygon."), [`bbox`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a06e8f55ebcb8cf814bbc5df5d4288312 "Computes the bounding box of the convex polygon."), [`begin`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#ac3d6626a1da2b4dcb4723679ba2d36b4 "Returns a constant iterator to the beginning of vertices."), [`size`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#af571fa35d1459bf957d1e57a7a6dab54 "Returns the number of vertices in the convex polygon."), [`squaredDistance`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a49b89d1d91978c3dcfee6f206f9b00e2 "Returns the squared Euclidean distance to the given shape."), [`squaredHausdorffDistance`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a35a594af46447f78aac923bfeeb43bc2 "Returns the squared Hausdorff distance to the given shape."), [`twiceArea`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a0cb5bbaafdd13d462ebda639e6bbc8d3 "Computes twice the area of the convex polygon."), [`vertices`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#ad5be32b0077d06c77f97954d1213792a "Returns the vertices of the convex polygon."), [`verticesCentroid`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a18faa3ce367b7e75a14ad67cb994148d "Computes the centroid of the vertex set."), [`verticesContain`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a9017a5bd874b74b80bd70f97f4444f40 "Checks if the vertices list contains the given point.").

### Halfplane Intersection

The class template [`HalfplaneIntersection`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1HalfplaneIntersection.html "Intersection of closed half-planes; convex but possibly unbounded or empty.") represents the intersection of a finite set of closed half-planes: a convex region that, unlike [`Convex`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html "Closed convex polygon stored by its vertices."), may be unbounded (a wedge, a strip, a half-plane, or the whole plane) and may be empty. Its vertices are generally not representable in the coordinate type of the defining half-planes: integer half-planes routinely bound regions with rational vertices, so the constructive accessors take a result-type template parameter in the usual way (`k.vertex<pgl::Rational<int64_t>>(i)` is exact).

The half-planes are stored sorted counterclockwise by boundary direction, with no redundant half-plane and at most one half-plane per direction. A default-constructed [`HalfplaneIntersection`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1HalfplaneIntersection.html "Intersection of closed half-planes; convex but possibly unbounded or empty.") is the **whole plane** (the intersection of no half-planes) — the opposite convention of `Convex()`, which is the empty set. It can also be constructed from a range of half-planes, or from a [`Halfplane`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html "Closed half-plane defined by an oriented boundary line."), [`Rectangle`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html "Axis-aligned rectangle stored by minimum and maximum corners."), [`Triangle`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html "Closed triangle stored by three vertices."), or [`Convex`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html "Closed convex polygon stored by its vertices.").

A half-plane intersection `k` has methods such as:

- [`k.insert(h)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1HalfplaneIntersection.html#ac1856ebc53281385a995a6bdd43461ec "Intersects the region with one more half-plane."): Intersects the region with one more half-plane. The half-plane is discarded (returning false) when it is redundant; when it empties the region, the region switches to a sticky empty state; otherwise it is stored and the stored half-planes it makes redundant are removed. Amortized $O(\log n)$ comparisons.
- [`k.isEmpty()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1HalfplaneIntersection.html#acf256e2436fe1e477b4e652e4c7ac098 "Returns whether the region is the empty set."), [`k.isPlane()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1HalfplaneIntersection.html#afece20a10fa322f6450ad3c4691aefba "Returns whether the region is the whole plane (no half-planes)."), [`k.isBounded()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1HalfplaneIntersection.html#a79271b52491bf5ce89ab26d5ef574e92 "Returns whether the region is bounded."), [`k.isDegenerate()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1HalfplaneIntersection.html#a7f912f08d9342e92565a52bab98d4ab7 "Returns whether the region has empty interior (it is empty or lower-dimensional: a line, ray, segment, or point)."): State queries. A degenerate region has empty interior (a line, ray, segment, or point built from touching constraints); it remains fully supported by the predicates.
- `k.vertex<R>(i)`, `k.vertices<R>()`, [`k.vertexCount()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1HalfplaneIntersection.html#aa12330e03c05998ca53a4260cfa2a2e8 "Returns the number of vertices of the region."): The implicit vertices, counterclockwise for bounded regions.
- `k.edge<R>(i)`: The boundary contribution of half-plane `i` as a `std::variant` of [`Segment`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html "Unoriented closed segment between two endpoints plus optional segment label."), [`Ray`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Ray.html "Half-infinite line starting from one source point plus optional ray label."), or [`Line`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Line.html "Unoriented infinite line.").
- `k.bbox<R>()`, [`k.fbox()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1HalfplaneIntersection.html#aed55af0630fb978ad186a3ff06c650eb "Computes the floating-point bounding box of the region."): Bounding box; throws `std::logic_error` when the region is empty or unbounded. With an integer result type the box is rounded outward so it always encloses the region.
- `k.asConvex<R>()`: The region as a [`Convex`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html "Closed convex polygon stored by its vertices."); throws when unbounded.
- [`k.intersection(h)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1HalfplaneIntersection.html#a5da65ca307d0a84216446524765a7a33 "Returns the intersection of the two shapes (A ∩ B), empty when they are disjoint."): Intersecting with a [`Halfplane`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Halfplane.html "Closed half-plane defined by an oriented boundary line."), [`Rectangle`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html "Axis-aligned rectangle stored by minimum and maximum corners."), [`Triangle`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html "Closed triangle stored by three vertices."), [`Convex`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html "Closed convex polygon stored by its vertices."), or another [`HalfplaneIntersection`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1HalfplaneIntersection.html "Intersection of closed half-planes; convex but possibly unbounded or empty.") returns another [`HalfplaneIntersection`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1HalfplaneIntersection.html "Intersection of closed half-planes; convex but possibly unbounded or empty."), so the type is closed under these operations and the result is exact (no coordinate divisions).

If the region has $n$ half-planes, then:

- [`k.contains(p)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1HalfplaneIntersection.html#a0339b8574bb578ad289a1a023fae4976 "Tests whether this shape contains the other shape (A ⊇ B)."), [`k.intersects(s)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1HalfplaneIntersection.html#ad5ce4d362f769f6da4b6e41237f86450 "Tests whether this shape and the other shape intersect (A ∩ B ≠ ∅)."), and the other predicates against points, segments, lines, rays, and half-planes take $O(\log n)$ time ([`separates`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1HalfplaneIntersection.html#aac1cadcd51404840ccd7051666626b74 "Tests whether removing this shape disconnects the other shape (B∖A is disconnected).") against a half-plane takes $O(n)$).
- [`k.insert(h)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1HalfplaneIntersection.html#ac1856ebc53281385a995a6bdd43461ec "Intersects the region with one more half-plane.") takes $O(\log n)$ amortized comparisons (plus vector element moves).
- [`k.isBounded()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1HalfplaneIntersection.html#a79271b52491bf5ce89ab26d5ef574e92 "Returns whether the region is bounded.") and [`k.vertexCount()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1HalfplaneIntersection.html#aa12330e03c05998ca53a4260cfa2a2e8 "Returns the number of vertices of the region.") take $O(n)$ time.

Equality compares the stored half-planes: for full-dimensional regions the non-redundant half-planes are a canonical function of the point set, so this is geometric equality; for lower-dimensional (degenerate) regions the representation is not unique and equality is representational.

[`HalfplaneIntersection`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1HalfplaneIntersection.html "Intersection of closed half-planes; convex but possibly unbounded or empty.") is an alternative of the polymorphic [`Shape`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Shape.html "Runtime variant wrapper over the supported primitive shapes.") class and can be drawn on a [canvas](canvas.md), which clips the region to the visible viewport and strokes only its real boundary edges. Note that `Shape::get`, `Shape::operator[]`, and `Shape::index` throw for this alternative, since its indexable elements are half-planes rather than points.
